title: How Many Crores to FIRE in India? Honest Math From a Guy Who Did It at 33
meta_title: How Many Crores to FIRE in India? Honest Math From a Guy Who Did It at 33
subtitle: A 5-step builder that gives every cost its own inflation: healthcare, education, parents, housing, and your own life
description: How many crores do you actually need to FIRE in India? A 5-step builder from someone who retired at 33, giving each cost its own inflation (healthcare 12%, education 10%, lifestyle 6%) instead of a single lazy multiplier.

faqs.question: How many crores do you need to FIRE in India?
faqs.answer: It depends almost entirely on your monthly spend, your city, and your family. A lean couple in a tier 2 city can land near ₹3 crore, while a family of 4 in a metro with international schooling can cross ₹15 crore. The builder in this article assembles your own number from every category rather than guessing an average.

faqs.question: Should Indians use 25x or 33x for their FIRE number?
faqs.answer: Use more than the American 25x, and I plan my lifestyle at 33x, which is a 3% withdrawal. The sharper answer is that no single multiple fits everything, because healthcare inflates near 12% and education near 10% while everyday costs sit around 6%, so each category deserves its own multiple rather than one blended number.

faqs.question: Why does healthcare need a bigger multiplier in India?
faqs.answer: Because medical costs inflate at roughly 12% a year, faster than a portfolio typically grows, so a normal 33x does not keep up. You fund health as a dedicated buffer, closer to 35 times your current annual premium, and you keep raising your cover as you age.

faqs.question: How do you account for supporting parents in a FIRE plan?
faqs.answer: As a finite stream, not a forever cost. Parent support inflates around 7% and runs for a limited horizon, so it costs roughly 16 times the annual amount rather than 33. On top of that, insure your parents while they are still insurable, or hold a ₹30 lakh to ₹50 lakh medical buffer if they are not.

faqs.question: How much does raising a child change the FIRE number in India?
faqs.answer: A lot, because education inflates at about 10% and runs for roughly 22 years per child. Streamed at its own inflation, a child's education costs about 22 times a single year of fees, so solid local private near ₹1 lakh a year, premium private near ₹3 lakh, and international near ₹8 lakh each imply very different corpuses.

faqs.question: Should I calculate my FIRE number in today's rupees or future rupees?
faqs.answer: Either works, and mixing them is the mistake. In today's rupees, apply the multipliers to your current costs and pair them with real returns. In future rupees, inflate your spending first and pair it with full nominal returns. Both give the same answer in different clothes.
